questions.question: Which of the following provisions of the Indian Constitution has been implemented in West Bengal?
options: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1973
The Scheduled Castes and Scheduled Tribes (Prevention of Atrocities) Act, 1989
questions.explanation: The Panchayat (Extension to Scheduled Areas) Act, 1973 is implemented in Scheduled Areas of the country including West Bengal.

questions.question: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 73rd Constitutional Amendment Act provides for the establishment of 3-tier Panchayat Raj in the States.

questions.question: The West Bengal Public Service Commission is a constitutional body established under which Article of the Indian Constitution?
questions.explanation: The West Bengal Public Service Commission is established under Article 315 of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Which of the following is a feature of the West Bengal Panchayat Raj system?
questions.explanation: The West Bengal Panchayat Raj system has a three-tier panchayat structure.

questions.question: The 42nd Amendment Act of the Indian Constitution deals with which of the following?
questions.explanation: The 42nd Amendment Act made several changes to the Emergency Provisions of the Indian Constitution.

questions.question: Who led the Swadeshi Movement in Bengal in the early 20th century?
questions.explanation: Rash Behari Bose played a significant role in the Bengal uprising.

questions.question: Which famous Bengali poet and writer was a key figure in the Indian freedom struggle?
questions.explanation: Bankim Chandra Chattopadhyay was a prominent writer and thinker during the freedom struggle.

questions.question: What was the name of the first Indian war of independence against the British in 1857?
questions.explanation: The Indian Rebellion of 1857 marked the beginning of India's fight for freedom.

questions.question: Who was a prominent figure in the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal?
questions.explanation: C.R. Das played a crucial role in the Non-Cooperation Movement in Bengal.

questions.question: The partition of which Bengal district led to widespread communal violence in 1947?
questions.explanation: The partition of the Jessore district in 1947 led to severe communal tensions.

questions.question: Which river originates from the Himalayas and flows through the State of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges originates from the Himalayas.

questions.question: What is the name of the island city located off the coast of Kolkata, West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The island city near Kolkata is known as Gangasagar.

questions.question: Which of the following mountain ranges is located in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sikkim Himalayas are located in Darjeeling district.

questions.question: What is the name of the largest delta in the world, located in the State of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Sundarbans Delta is the largest delta in the world.

questions.question: Which of the following passes is located in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Jelep La pass is located in Darjeeling district.

questions.question: Which city in West Bengal has been selected as the first Smart City under the Centre's Smart City Mission?
questions.explanation: The city of Siliguri was selected in the first round of the Smart City Challenge.

questions.question: Which of the following schemes was launched by the West Bengal government in 2022 to support farmers?
questions.explanation: Krishi Sanjeevani is a scheme introduced by West Bengal to provide financial assistance to farmers.

questions.question: Who is the current Chief Minister of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Mamata Banerjee has been serving as the Chief Minister of West Bengal since 2011.

questions.question: What is the name of the river that originates from the Himalayas and flows through the state of West Bengal?
questions.explanation: The Ganges River originates from the Himalayas and flows through West Bengal.

questions.question: Which of the following is a major port in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: Haldia Port is a major port located in the state of West Bengal.

questions.question: What is the main objective of the 'Swasthya Sathi' scheme launched by the West Bengal Government?
options: To provide cashless treatment to all citizens
To provide health insurance to all citizens
To provide free diagnostic services to all citizens
To provide Ayurvedic treatment to all citizens
questions.explanation: It is a health insurance scheme.

questions.question: What is the main focus of the 'Yubak Bunyo Yojana' scheme in West Bengal?
options: Empowering women through education and skills
questions.explanation: It is a scheme for women empowerment.

questions.question: What is the name of the scheme launched by the West Bengal Government to support the livelihood of tea garden workers?
questions.explanation: It is a scheme for tea garden workers.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key component of the 'Sabooj Sathi' scheme in West Bengal?
questions.explanation: It provides free bicycles to students.

questions.question: What is the main aim of the 'Banglar Krishi' scheme launched by the West Bengal Government?
options: To promote organic farming in the state
To improve irrigation facilities in the state
To increase crop yield in the state
To reduce chemical use in farming
questions.explanation: It promotes organic farming.

questions.question: A merchant in Kolkata buys 80 kg of potatoes at Rs. 15 per kg. If he sells the potatoes at 10% profit, then find the profit percentage he has achieved over the cost price.
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is the selling price percentage minus the cost price percentage.

questions.question: A man buys 2 shares, each of face value Rs. 100, at Rs. 120 and sells them at a 10% profit. Calculate the percentage profit he makes on his investment.
questions.explanation: Profit percentage is calculated by taking the selling price percentage minus the cost price percentage.

questions.question: A company in Siliguri has a profit of 25% on its sales. If it earns a profit of Rs. 25,000 from a sale of Rs. 1,00,000, then calculate the net profit percentage of the company.
questions.explanation: Net profit percentage is the profit percentage minus the selling price percentage.

questions.question: Mr. Roy buys a car for Rs. 2,00,000 and sells it at a 15% loss. Calculate the selling price of the car.
questions.explanation: Selling price is the face value minus the loss percentage.

questions.question: A person invests Rs. 8,000 in a business that gives a 12% return. Find the profit he makes in a year.
questions.explanation: Profit is the return on investment.

questions.question: A pipe can fill 1/4th of a tank in 15 minutes. How long will it take to fill the entire tank?
questions.explanation: Total time taken is 4 times the given time.

questions.question: Three friends A, B and C can finish painting a house in 6, 8 and 12 hours respectively. How long will it take for all of them to paint the house together?
questions.explanation: Combined work rate is the sum of individual work rates.

questions.question: A tap can fill 2/3rd of a tank in 24 minutes. If the tap is closed and another tap is opened, which can fill the tank in 12 minutes, how long will it take to fill the entire tank?
questions.explanation: Combined work rate is the sum of individual work rates.

questions.question: A man can do a piece of work in 20 days. He works for 10 days and then his wife helps him. How long will it take for both of them together to complete the remaining work?
questions.explanation: Combined work rate is the sum of individual work rates.

questions.question: A pipe can fill a tank in 30 minutes, but due to a leak, it takes 3 hours to fill the tank. What is the rate of leakage in terms of the volume of water filled by the pipe in one hour?
questions.explanation: Rate of leakage is the difference between the two rates.

questions.question: A train travels from Kolkata to Siliguri at an average speed of 60 km/h. How much time will it take to cover a distance of 240 km?
questions.explanation: The time is calculated by dividing the distance by the speed.

questions.question: A bus covers a distance of 480 km in 8 hours. What is the speed of the bus in km/h?
questions.explanation: The speed is calculated by dividing the distance by the time.

questions.question: A person travels from Kolkata to Howrah at a speed of 25 km/h and returns at a speed of 30 km/h. What is the average speed of the person for the entire trip?
questions.explanation: The average speed is calculated by dividing the total distance by the total time.

questions.question: A car travels from Barasat to Jessore with a uniform speed. If the distance between the two places is 150 km and the time taken is 5 hours, what is the speed of the car?
questions.explanation: The speed is calculated by dividing the distance by the time.

questions.question: A motorcyclist travels from Durgapur to Asansol at an average speed of 70 km/h. If he covers a distance of 140 km in 2 hours, what is the remaining distance to be covered?
questions.explanation: The remaining distance is calculated by subtracting the covered distance from the total distance.

questions.question: A book store is offering 15% discount on all books. If the original price of a book is Rs. 350, what will be the price after the discount?
questions.explanation: 15% of Rs. 350 is Rs. 52.50, so the price after the discount is Rs. 350 - Rs. 52.50.

questions.question: If in a code language 'Friend' is written as 'ENEMA', 'Lion' as 'NIGSI', 'Bird' as 'KFDUA', then what is the code for 'King'?
questions.explanation: The pattern of encoding is the first letter of the word and the last letter of the word.

questions.question: A man started a business with Rs. 20000. After 3 months, another person joined him with Rs. 30000. What is the ratio of their investments?
questions.explanation: The ratio of their investments is the product of the amount and the time period.

questions.question: In a code, '25' is written as 'AB', '52' as 'DC', '13' as 'FE'. What will be the code for '38'?
questions.explanation: The pattern of encoding is the first letter of the number and the last letter of the number.

questions.question: If in a code language 'Father' is written as 'BABA', 'Mother' as 'MAMA', 'Brother' as 'AKAK', then what is the code for 'Sister'?
questions.explanation: The pattern of encoding is the repetition of the first letter of the word.

questions.question: A person is at a place from where the peak of Darjeeling is visible. If he walks 5 km towards north, then walks 7 km to the east and finally walks 3 km towards south, in which direction is the Darjeeling peak now?
questions.explanation: The person's final position is to the east of the initial position.

questions.question: A man is standing at a point P in Kolkata. If he moves 10 km towards south, then 15 km towards east and finally 8 km towards north, in which direction is he standing now?
questions.explanation: The man's final position is to the south-east of the initial position.

questions.question: A person is standing at a place in West Bengal. He walks 2 km towards north, then 4 km towards east and finally 1 km towards south. What is the shortest distance from his final position to the point where he started?
questions.explanation: The person ended up 1 km south and 4 km east of the starting point.

questions.question: A man is walking in a field in Malda district. He walks 2.5 km to the north, then 3.5 km to the east, and finally 1.5 km to the south. What is his final bearing?
questions.explanation: The man's final position is to the north-east of the initial position.

questions.question: A person is standing at a place in Howrah. He moves 12 km to the south, then 8 km to the east and finally 4 km to the north. How far is he from the starting point now?
questions.explanation: The person is 8 km south and 8 km east of the starting point.

questions.question: Identify the next number in the series: 2, 6, 12, 20, 30, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 4, 6, 8, 10, ...

questions.question: Which of the following numbers should replace the question mark in the series: 3, 6, 9, ?, 12, 15?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 3.

questions.question: Identify the next letter in the series: AB, DE, GH, ?, JK, MN?
questions.explanation: The pattern is shifting 3 letters forward in the alphabet.

questions.question: Which of the following numbers should replace the question mark in the series: 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is squaring the numbers.

questions.question: Identify the next number in the series: 5, 10, 20, 35, 55, ?
questions.explanation: The pattern is adding 5, 10, 15, ...
